qualifying_org_definition: 501(c)(3) or 501(c)(4) nonprofit, federal/state/local government agency, or church/place of worship — per PA 1938 Section III agency certification language
verification_instrument: PA 1938 (Community Service / Volunteer Verification Form, 3/20 edition)
hours_formula_detail: SNAP allotment ÷ $7.25 federal minimum wage; example: $292 allotment ÷ $7.25 = ~41 hours/month for a single-person household. The CAO computes the figure and records it on the Employment Development Plan; Section II of PA 1938 captures weekly/monthly hours from that plan. The flat 80-hour standard applies to employment/training activities but NOT to community service/volunteer work under PA ABAWD rules.
accepts_outofstate_detail: PA 1938 Section III certification language and the PA DHS SNAP work requirements page specify only that the organization must be a 501(c)(3), 501(c)(4), government agency, or church meeting applicable laws — no geographic restriction on the nonprofit's location is stated anywhere in PA policy. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) does not restrict qualifying organizations by state, so out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally acceptable absent an explicit PA restriction.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide. PA DHS states: 'Pennsylvania no longer qualifies for waivers of these rules by county or municipal jurisdiction because of a change in the federal law.' OBBBA raised the threshold for area waivers to 10% unemployment, which no PA county meets. One third-party source (benefitscope.com) noted Lancaster and Lebanon cities may have delayed implementation until September 2026, but this was not corroborated by the official PA DHS page and may reflect the pre-OBBBA waiver expiration timeline.
submission_deadline: Within 10 days of receipt for initial PA 1938 form (mailed or faxed to CAO or Work Ready contractor). Changes in participation must be reported within 10 days of the change occurring. Form covers up to 6 months; a new form is required every 6 months.
notes: Pennsylvania requires a mandatory Employment Development Plan (EDP) at the County Assistance Office (CAO) before volunteer hours can count toward ABAWD compliance — the PA 1938 form's instructions state explicitly that the 'Required Monthly Hours section is completed by the CAO or E&T contractor based on the hours computed by the CAO and listed on the Employment Development Plan.' Hours are county-determined (CAO sets the required monthly floor based on the recipient's SNAP grant divided by minimum wage), not self-reported. The org-signed PA 1938 form is the verification instrument, but it cannot function independently without the prior CAO/EDP assignment step, placing PA firmly in Tier 2. ABAWD is enforced statewide as of November 2025 with no county waivers remaining under OBBBA. EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula confirmed as workfare (allotment ÷ $7.25) for volunteer/community service track — CLS Philadelphia example shows ~41 hrs/month for a single-person $292 allotment; flat 80-hr standard does not apply to this track. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: PA 1938 and DHS page are silent on geography; only 501(c)(3)/501(c)(4)/gov/church status required, so federal default (any qualifying org) applies — classified 'silent.' (3) Geographic waivers: none statewide per PA DHS; OBBBA eliminated county-level flexibility below 10% unemployment. (4) Submission UX: mail or fax PA 1938 to CAO within 10 days of receipt; changes within 10 days of change; form valid 6 months / renewed at recertification. (5) Form fields extracted directly from PA 1938 PDF text: org_name, org_address, org_phone, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, recipient_DOB, recipient_address, hours_by_day_or_week (weekly schedule grid), hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date. No EIN or case number field on form (last 4 SSN used instead); no notarization required.
